summary_one_line: Input must decode to a Khmer Coeng (K_Cat(H)) glyph with <=2 prior coengs at a non-final position so the syllable reorder loop takes the true arm.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Bytes that decode to a Khmer text run containing a Coeng codepoint (U+17D2, classified as K_Cat(H)) positioned so info[i].khmer_category()==K_Cat(H) holds with num_coengs<=2 and i+1<end, i.e. a non-final Coeng glyph inside a consonant/broken-cluster syllable.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive-discovered winners carry repeated 17/d2 byte runs (e.g. seed 1 d2 17 .. d2 00 1a 17) that decode to Coeng-bearing Khmer syllables; on the W role the H/Coeng arm at 246-274 is taken and the branch hits 203 times in 10/10 trials.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog-discovered losers instead drive the input into the sibling VPre arm (line 277 else-if, tagged [L] 278-283) so the K_Cat(H) true side is never satisfied; the loser blocks the branch in 10/10 trials with hitcount 0.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The winner is naive (plain havoc byte stack), not cmplog. The compared operand K_Cat(H) is a Unicode-property-derived glyph category computed inside harfbuzz, not a literal present in the input bytes, so cmplog's input-to-state CMP-operand substitution has no operand to swap and gains no traction; broad havoc byte mutation is what stumbles onto the Coeng-producing byte arrangement. cmplog's comparison-solving effort is wasted/misdirected here, so the havoc-only baseline (naive) wins.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker condition tests the Khmer glyph category K_Cat(H) (Coeng) under a coeng-count and position guard.
naive is the decisive winner and cmplog is the decisive loser; the delta technique is I2S.
naive resolves all 10 trials while cmplog blocks all 10, with winner hitcount 203 vs loser 0.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=203  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: On the winner role the H/Coeng arm body (246-274) is executed, while on the loser role the sibling VPre arm (277-283) is taken.
Winner-resolving seeds carry repeated 17 / d2 byte runs consistent with Khmer codepoints such as U+17D2 (Coeng) in little-endian.
The winning seed was produced by a plain havoc byte mutator (BytesExpandMutator), characteristic of naive's mutation stack.
cmplog's I2SRandReplace substitutes one CMP operand for the other by scanning the input for matching byte sequences.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: naive's mutators are purely the havoc/token stack with no I2S introspection.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is havoc-only (the decisive WINNER is naive, not a cmplog/vpc fuzzer), so the I2S-floor lineage check does not apply to the winner's technique. For completeness I ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 5920 --role W --fuzzer naive --trial 1 --seed 0d431e94a78d2b78`: the chain is dominated by havoc ops (BytesExpandMutator, QwordAddMutator, CrossoverInsertMutator, etc.) with a single dash row at depth 10; per the MECHANISM CONTEXT caveat the dash=I2S signal is only valid for cmplog/vpc, and naive uses plain LineageMutator, so that deep dash row is a seeding/import artifact and not I2S evidence. This confirms the winner reached the branch via havoc, not I2S.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the K_Cat(H) gate is replaced by a direct comparison of an input byte field against a fixed literal would, if cmplog then beats naive at reaching the true side, refute the claim that the Coeng category is opaque to I2S substitution.

weakest_evidence_point: The byte-level claim that 17/d2 runs decode specifically to U+17D2 Coeng rather than any other Khmer codepoint is inferred from the source category gate, not directly confirmed by a decoder trace.
